purpose: Machine-readable register for future WellFit agent follow-ups detected after documentation, registry, or PR outcome review. It keeps follow-up detection inside the existing agent memory loop without creating duplicate TODO files, architecture, product logic, or parallel systems.

followUpCategories.label: API proposal
followUpCategories.description: A future or changed API surface is proposed in docs/registers and needs alignment with project-register/apis.json before runtime changes.

followUpCategories.label: Firestore collection proposal
followUpCategories.description: A future Firestore collection, rule, index, emulator scenario, or data-flow proposal is mentioned and needs planning/review before writes.

followUpCategories.label: Route added or changed
followUpCategories.description: An app route or route register entry changed and may need route inventory, screenshots, or UI shell stability checks.

followUpCategories.label: Register changed
followUpCategories.description: A project-register file changed and may need WORK_MAP.md, TODO_INDEX.md, and memory-loop synchronization.

followUpCategories.label: Feedback plan added
followUpCategories.description: A feedback intake, analysis, or analytics plan changed and may need review against existing user-feedback and privacy-safe planning registers.

followUpCategories.label: Mission or economy touched
followUpCategories.description: Mission, XP, points, reward, buddy, anti-cheat, or economy-adjacent areas were mentioned or touched and must remain preview/planning unless explicitly approved.

followUpCategories.label: UI route touched
followUpCategories.description: A visible route, component, page, or navigation-adjacent file changed and may need route audits or screenshots when runnable.

followUpCategories.label: Governance changed
followUpCategories.description: Agent workflow, TODO governance, quality gate, or memory-loop files changed and need register/TODO alignment checks.

requiredReviewRules: Do not create duplicate TODO, architecture, API, route, feature, data-model, or economy systems; continue in existing mapped files.
High and critical follow-ups are never auto-approved by scripts and require human review or planning-only handling.
Critical product/compliance areas require explicit human approval and a review plan before implementation.
Follow-up entries should cite the source PR, changed files, checks, risk level, and the single safest next recommended task.
Product logic, token/NFT/wallet/payment/betting/reward authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, and compliance behavior must not be modified by follow-up detection.

examples.nextRecommendedTask: Run npm run agent:quality-gate and keep WORK_MAP.md/TODO_INDEX.md synchronized.

examples.source: A planning doc proposes a future feedback collection or Firestore rule scenario.
examples.nextRecommendedTask: Create planning-only review notes and do not write runtime Firestore logic without explicit approval.

examples.source: A PR mentions mission rewards, points, XP, anti-cheat, or economy APIs.
examples.nextRecommendedTask: Classify against risk-classifier.json and keep authority server-side or draft/preview only.
